prevalence.value: common seasonal illness; ~10-20% of infants affected annually
prevalence.trend: seasonal peaks; severe burden in infants and preterm babies

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case in severe disease; overall pediatric burden significant
burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; higher in high-risk infants and limited-resource settings

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis (wheezing, cough, increased work of breathing) in infants during RSV season; pulse oximetry; exclude pneumonia; RSV testing not always required

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: hundreds of thousands of infant cases/season
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Good with supportive care; severe cases require hospital/oxygen support

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in APS/ER; RSV testing used variably in hospitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care: hydration, nasal suction, oxygen if hypoxic; avoid routine bronchodilators/antibiotics unless indicated; ICU for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Seasonal surges overwhelming pediatric wards and unequal access to pediatric ICU in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established pediatric guidelines and broad hospital network for supportive care in SUS
